title: Chrysophyllum litseiflorum
taxonomy: Chrysophyllum litseiflorum is a species of plant that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. This family is a part of the order Ebenales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Ebenales is further classified under the clade Asterids, a large and diverse group of angiosperms. The clade Asterids is itself a part of the larger clade Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. Overall, Chrysophyllum litseiflorum's classification reflects its position as a member of the Sapotaceae family within the broader group of flowering plants.
uses: Chrysophyllum litseiflorum has been reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. Its bark and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. In some regions, the plant is used to treat digestive problems and to reduce inflammation. The plant's extracts have also been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential candidate for the development of new medicines. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Chrysophyllum litseiflorum.
